title: triticum caesium
side_effects: Triticum caesium can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged exposure to the plant's caesium content may also cause damage to the nervous system, resulting in muscle weakness and tremors. In severe cases, improper use of Triticum caesium can lead to cardiac problems, including arrhythmias and decreased blood pressure. Furthermore, consuming large amounts of the plant can cause kidney damage and other long-term health issues.
